subject: a love that ended over a text message
lyrics:
[Verse]
The morning came in soft and gray
My coffee cold, my words away
Three little lines upon the glass
A quiet way to let me pass

[Verse]
No trembling voice, no closing door
Just pixels fading on the floor
The ocean hums the same old song
While something gentle slips along

[Chorus]
You typed goodbye in pale blue light
And sent the summer into night
No melody to say it slow
Just here it is, and there you go

[Verse]
I read it twice, I read it through
The sand still warm, the sky still blue
The waves don't care how love departs
They roll the same on broken hearts

[Bridge]
So I will hum a lazy tune
Beneath a tired and tender moon
And let the tide erase the screen
The softest end I've ever seen

[Chorus]
You typed goodbye in pale blue light
And sent the summer into night
No melody to say it slow
Just here it is, and there you go

subject: driving past your old apartment at night
lyrics:
[Verse]
Midnight on the same block, engine humming low
Window up, music down, headlights on slow
Third floor on the left, that light used to be ours
Now it's somebody's silhouette counting different hours
I ain't stop, I just slow-rolled, foot off the gas
Memories in the rearview, moving too fast
We painted that kitchen, argued in that hall
Now I'm a stranger to the bricks, that's all

[Chorus]
Driving past your old place when the city's asleep
Got the ghosts in the passenger, promises I couldn't keep
Green light, keep it moving, don't you look too long
Some roads you only travel in a quiet little song

[Verse]
Mailbox still dented from the night that we moved
Same cracked sidewalk where we found our groove
I could circle this corner till the sun come up
But you don't live here no more, time to give it up
So I tap the brake once, then I let it ride
Leave the block in the mirror, keep the love inside

[Bridge]
Lights change, blocks change, people rearrange
I'm just passing through a place that I can't reclaim
One last look, then the avenue is gone
And I drive into the dark with the radio on

[Chorus]
Driving past your old place when the city's asleep
Got the ghosts in the passenger, promises I couldn't keep
Green light, keep it moving, don't you look too long
Some roads you only travel in a quiet little song

subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
lyrics:
[Verse]
Three in the mornin' on a Tennessee line
Diesel and coffee and a heart out of time
I got a quarter and a payphone glow
Callin' you up from the road I don't know

[Chorus]
Hello darlin', it's your travelin' man
Far from the porch and the wedding band
The wheels keep turnin' but my heart stays home
Three in the mornin' and I'm callin' you alone

[Verse]
Hear the baby cryin' somewhere down the hall
Wish I could hold you and answer that call
Mountains in the windshield, white lines in my eyes
A thousand more miles 'neath these old western skies

[Verse]
You tell me 'bout the garden and the dog gettin' gray
I tell you 'bout the sunrise I'll chase down today
The fiddle on the radio sounds just like you
Lonesome and pretty and achin' for true

[Bridge]
So keep the light on in the kitchen for me
I'll be a shadow comin' home through the trees
Friday or Sunday, whenever I can
God and the highway will carry your man

[Chorus]
Hello darlin', it's your travelin' man
Far from the porch and the wedding band
The wheels keep turnin' but my heart stays home
Three in the mornin' and I'm callin' you alone

subject: a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
lyrics:
[Verse]
A blue neon hum on a rest-stop wall
Dime in the slot and I'm makin' the call
Three in the mornin', the world's gone to sleep
Just me and a dial tone, promises to keep

[Chorus]
Hey there, baby, it's your midnight man
Rollin' through the dark as long as I can
Your voice like a saxophone, low and warm
Carryin' me soft through the highway storm

[Verse]
The coffee's gone bitter, the cigarette's done
Mile marker shadows and a far-off run
You yawn and you whisper, you say my name
And somethin' in the static starts to feel like a flame

[Bridge]
The city's all velvet and the diesel sings
Love on a wire is a fragile thing
But I'll hum your number like a favorite tune
And drive toward your window 'neath a workin' moon

[Chorus]
Hey there, baby, it's your midnight man
Rollin' through the dark as long as I can
Your voice like a saxophone, low and warm
Carryin' me soft through the highway storm

subject: an unrequited love
lyrics:
[Verse]
I watch you cross the smoke-blue floor
A flame I cannot hold
You give your hand to someone else
And leave my heart in cold

[Verse]
Every step you take is fire
Every turn a knife
I burn beside the velvet wall
And dream you into life

[Chorus]
Dance with me, but only in my mind
Love that I was never meant to find
You are the song I'll never get to play
The rose that turns its petals away

[Verse]
The bandoneon, it weeps for me
A slow and bleeding sound
You laugh into another's eyes
While I stay nailed to the ground

[Bridge]
Give me one illusion, just one night
Let me pull you close beneath the light
Then wake me to the empty, aching truth
That I have only ever danced with you

[Chorus]
Dance with me, but only in my mind
Love that I was never meant to find
You are the song I'll never get to play
The rose that turns its petals away

subject: the quiet after a fight with someone you love
lyrics:
[Verse]
Door just closed but it didn't slam
We ran out of words and we ran out of damn
Sittin' on the couch 'bout a foot apart
Loud as a drum is my beat-up heart

[Chorus]
Ooh, the quiet after the storm
Two cold hands tryna find the warm
No more shoutin', no more noise
Just the silence between two voices

[Verse]
Clock on the wall got a heavy tick
Pride's a stone and it's twice as thick
I wanna reach but my hand stays still
Love's on the table gettin' cold and chill

[Verse]
Your foot taps soft, mine taps too
Funny how the silence knows what to do
Somewhere in the hush there's a melody
Callin' you back, callin' you to me

[Bridge]
So I slide a little closer on the groove
First one to soften gotta make the move
Take your hand, baby, feel the bass come down
Let the quiet turn the whole thing 'round

[Chorus]
Ooh, the quiet after the storm
Two cold hands tryna find the warm
No more shoutin', no more noise
Just the silence between two voices

subject: a panic attack on the subway
lyrics:
[Verse]
Fluorescent flicker, steel and glass
The tunnel swallows, the seconds pass
My heartbeat racing past the rails
Neon static where the signal fails

[Chorus]
Breathe in the dark, the city hums
My chest a drum the panic drums
Hold on, hold on through the screaming line
I'll surface into the light in time

[Verse]
The walls lean in, the air goes thin
A thousand strangers, locked within
I count the lights as they streak and blur
A blue electric pulse, a whir

[Bridge]
Next stop, next stop, just one more
The doors will open, I'll find the door
The wave will crest and the wave will break
And I'll remember how to wake

[Chorus]
Breathe in the dark, the city hums
My chest a drum the panic drums
Hold on, hold on through the screaming line
I'll surface into the light in time

subject: two rivals forced to dance together
lyrics:
[Verse]
You take my hand like you'd take a blade
Two enemies in a charade
The music starts, we cannot leave
A war disguised in what we weave

[Chorus]
Step for step, we wage this fight
Grace and venom under light
I hate the way you move so well
This perfect, furious farewell

[Verse]
Your eyes are knives, my smile is steel
We spin the lie that this is real
The crowd believes it's passion's art
They cannot hear the dueling heart

[Verse]
A twist, a dip, a measured sneer
You pull me close, you whisper near
One wrong step and one will fall
So we hold tight against the wall

[Bridge]
The bandoneon won't let us part
It binds the rivals, heart to heart
And somewhere in this bitter dance
We almost trade the war for chance

[Chorus]
Step for step, we wage this fight
Grace and venom under light
I hate the way you move so well
This perfect, furious farewell

subject: coming home from the war
lyrics:
[Verse]
The bus let me off where the cornfields start
Same crooked mailbox, same beat-up heart
I'm wearing the boots that walked through hell
And a quiet I'm not sure how to tell

[Chorus]
I'm home, I'm home, but I don't feel found
The ghosts came back on the same Greyhound
Hold me like you knew the man I was
Not the one the desert turned me to because

[Verse]
The screen door creaks like it always did
Mom's got the kettle, dad keeps it hid
The handshake too long, the hug too tight
Nobody asks what I saw last night

[Verse]
The town looks small and the sky looks wide
There's a stranger now living inside
I count the seconds, I flinch at the door
Learning the peace that I'm fighting for

[Bridge]
So plant me here in the ordinary days
The lawn, the rain, the small-town haze
Let the porch light teach me how to stay
And slowly bring the boy I lost away

[Chorus]
I'm home, I'm home, but I don't feel found
The ghosts came back on the same Greyhound
Hold me like you knew the man I was
Not the one the desert turned me to because

subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
lyrics:
[Verse]
Every night I climb these winding stairs
Trim the wick and breathe the salty air
I fold my heart in envelopes of foam
And send them out to where you call your home

[Chorus]
Oh, my love, my endless tide
I write you letters no one's read inside
I am the lamp and you're the deep
The only company my soul will keep

[Verse]
The gulls are postmen, the wind's my pen
I tell you secrets, again and again
You never answer, but you never leave
You wear my words on every wave you weave

[Verse]
My lantern burns for sailors lost at sea
But every beam, my dear, is meant for thee
Forty years of dusk, of ink, of light
Loving something larger than the night

[Bridge]
And when I'm gone they'll find the bottled scrolls
A keeper's heart in salt and silver folds
So carry me out where the deep ones dream
And read me one last letter, oh my sea

[Chorus]
Oh, my love, my endless tide
I write you letters no one's read inside
I am the lamp and you're the deep
The only company my soul will keep

subject: a grandmother's hands
lyrics:
[Verse]
Maple syrup mornings, flour on her sleeve
Hands like worn leather but soft as I believe
She braided all my troubles into Sunday hair
And hummed an old gospel into the kitchen air

[Chorus]
Those hands, those hands, they raised a world
Folded every prayer that I unfurled
Wrinkled like a river, warm as bread
I feel them on my shoulder, on my head

[Verse]
She sewed our names in quilts of faded blue
Held the family steady when the storms came through
Every line and callus told a story long
A lifetime of giving, a body made of song

[Verse]
Now her chair sits empty in the amber light
But I knead the dough the way she taught me right
And when I press my palms into the warming clay
I swear I feel her holding mine to stay

[Bridge]
Generations rest inside a single touch
The love she handed down, oh, it was so much
So I'll carry it forward, palm to palm to palm
Her hands inside my hands, keeping me calm

[Chorus]
Those hands, those hands, they raised a world
Folded every prayer that I unfurled
Wrinkled like a river, warm as bread
I feel them on my shoulder, on my head

subject: making it out of a dead-end block
lyrics:
[Verse]
Rain on the window, beat tape spin
Grew up where the dead end caves you in
Same three corners, same gray sky
But I kept a little dream that wouldn't die

[Chorus]
Made it off the block, soft and slow
Left the only world I used to know
Dusty old loop, a tired piano key
This is the sound of getting free

[Verse]
Mama worked doubles, I studied the night
Wrote my way out by the streetlamp light
They said stay put, said the block was fate
But I packed up my future and I didn't wait

[Verse]
Now the city skyline trades places with the gray
Still I tip my cap to the cracks in the way
'Cause the dead end taught me how to find a door
And appreciate the things I'm grateful for

[Bridge]
Vinyl crackle, memories fade
The block made the man, the man got made
I send a little something back to the street
Where I first learned to ride a broken beat

[Chorus]
Made it off the block, soft and slow
Left the only world I used to know
Dusty old loop, a tired piano key
This is the sound of getting free

subject: coming home from the war
lyrics:
[Verse]
I float through the doorway like a half-remembered song
The wallpaper's the same but I've been gone so long
Light pools on the floorboards, soft and underwater slow
I'm here but I am drifting somewhere only echoes go

[Chorus]
Come home, come home, the voices hum
Through the gauze of all the things I've become
Wrap me in the curtains, in the haze, in the light
And dissolve the desert out of my night

[Verse]
Your face is a photograph developing in rain
I reach across the years to touch your name again
The garden's overgrown, the sky's a wash of pearl
I'm learning to be gentle in this quiet, fragile world

[Bridge]
Fade me into morning, into linen, into blue
Let the soft and shapeless hours stitch me back to you
The war is just a ringing now, a far and fading tone
And slowly, slowly, slowly, I am coming home

[Chorus]
Come home, come home, the voices hum
Through the gauze of all the things I've become
Wrap me in the curtains, in the haze, in the light
And dissolve the desert out of my night

subject: the northern lights
lyrics:
[Verse]
Silence over snowfields, breath turns into white
Green begins to ripple at the ceiling of the night
No sound, no hurry, only slow unfolding fire
A curtain made of color rising higher, ever higher

[Chorus]
Lights, soft lights, across the polar dome
The sky remembers somewhere we call home
Fold over me, your veils of green and gold
A story older than the cold

[Verse]
The stars step back in wonder, the horizon starts to glow
A river of the heavens drifting silent, drifting slow
I lie upon the frozen ground and let it fill my eyes
The whole world holding still beneath the breathing skies

[Bridge]
No words can hold this, only stillness, only awe
The pulse of distant suns in everything I saw
And when the dawn erases them, I'll carry what they gave
The quiet, glowing wonder of the wave

[Chorus]
Lights, soft lights, across the polar dome
The sky remembers somewhere we call home
Fold over me, your veils of green and gold
A story older than the cold

subject: the one that got away
lyrics:
[Verse]
Vinyl crackle, late September haze
Thinking 'bout you in a thousand little ways
We were almost something, almost a whole
Now you're just a warm spot missing from my soul

[Chorus]
The one that got away, on a mellow loop
Faded like the steam off a midnight cup
I hum your melody soft and low
The love I had to let go

[Verse]
Coffee shop corner where I saw you last
Dust on the needle, present turning past
I never said the words I should have said
Now they echo on a beat inside my head

[Verse]
Maybe in another life the timing's right
We catch the same train on the same blue night
But here it's just me and a tired refrain
A gentle little ache in a window full of rain

[Bridge]
So I let the tape spin, let it wear and fray
Some love's just a season, then it slips away
I wish you the morning, I wish you the sun
And I keep on missing the one

[Chorus]
The one that got away, on a mellow loop
Faded like the steam off a midnight cup
I hum your melody soft and low
The love I had to let go
